provenance.source_corpus: Response of thicket swamp species to soil moisture levels: Implications for restoration

source_spans.text: Response of thicket swamp species to soil moisture levels
These soil moisture differences produced a measurable effect on plant growth for six of the nine species tested
determine an estimated water use efficiency (WUEe)
A significant difference in root-to-shoot ratio (R:S) was found for eight of the species
Spiraea alba
Rosa palustris
Salix interior
Sambucus nigra
Solidago uliginosa
Solidago altissima
Cornus sericea
Salix discolor
Viburnum lentago

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:0eb14a094705
edges.original_sentence: These soil moisture differences produced a measurable effect on plant growth for six of the nine species tested.

source_spans.text: These soil moisture differences produced a measurable effect on plant growth for six of the nine species tested.

source_document.title: Response of thicket swamp species to soil moisture levels: Implications for restoration

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:0eb14a094705
edges.original_sentence: New growth was compared to mean stomatal conductivity to determine an estimated water use efficiency (WUEe), which demonstrated a montonal relationship for six of the species across the measured soil moisture regime.

source_spans.text: New growth was compared to mean stomatal conductivity to determine an estimated water use efficiency (WUEe), which demonstrated a montonal relationship for six of the species across the measured soil moisture regime.

source_document.title: Response of thicket swamp species to soil moisture levels: Implications for restoration

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:0eb14a094705
edges.original_sentence: A significant difference in root-to-shoot ratio (R:S) was found for eight of the species, with contrasting directions of change between the soil moisture treatments.

source_spans.text: A significant difference in root-to-shoot ratio (R:S) was found for eight of the species, with contrasting directions of change between the soil moisture treatments.

source_document.title: Response of thicket swamp species to soil moisture levels: Implications for restoration

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:a07727ae5318
edges.original_sentence: There was a ladder-like response in stem growth to soil moisture for Spiraea alba, with the Wet treatment resulting in nearly twice as much stem growth as the Control

source_spans.text: There was a ladder-like response in stem growth to soil moisture for Spiraea alba, with the Wet treatment resulting in nearly twice as much stem growth as the Control

source_document.title: Response of thicket swamp species to soil moisture levels: Implications for restoration

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:ea186e4f8e11
edges.original_sentence: Aboveground biomass of Rosa palustris exhibited the ladder-like increase in response to increased soil moisture, with Wet treatment mean biomass nearly twice the aboveground biomass of the Control treatment

source_spans.text: Aboveground biomass of Rosa palustris exhibited the ladder-like increase in response to increased soil moisture, with Wet treatment mean biomass nearly twice the aboveground biomass of the Control treatment

source_document.title: Response of thicket swamp species to soil moisture levels: Implications for restoration

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:87e4afc8c104
edges.original_sentence: Salix interior and Spiraea alba aboveground biomass was strongly restricted in the drier soils, with highly significant differences and effect sizes close to or greater than 2.0 between the two wetter treatments and the Control.

source_spans.text: Salix interior and Spiraea alba aboveground biomass was strongly restricted in the drier soils, with highly significant differences and effect sizes close to or greater than 2.0 between the two wetter treatments and the Control.

source_document.title: Response of thicket swamp species to soil moisture levels: Implications for restoration

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:8d0b0b268cb3
edges.original_sentence: Sambucus nigra stem growth peaked in the Moist treatment, with average new growth 50 and 100% greater than the drier and wetter treatments, respectively.

source_spans.text: Sambucus nigra stem growth peaked in the Moist treatment, with average new growth 50 and 100% greater than the drier and wetter treatments, respectively.
